title: PyTorch model — tensor shape mismatch and device error
author: ml-engineer
pr_description: Implements a convolutional image classifier model with adaptive pooling and a fully connected head.
diff:
diff --git a/model.py b/model.py
index eee5555..fff6666 100644
--- a/model.py
+++ b/model.py
@@ -1,4 +1,5 @@
 import torch
+import torch.nn as nn
 import torch.nn.functional as F
 
 
@@ -8,12 +9,33 @@ class ImageClassifier(nn.Module):
     def __init__(self, num_classes=10):
         super().__init__()
         self.conv1 = nn.Conv2d(3, 32, kernel_size=3, padding=1)
+        self.conv2 = nn.Conv2d(32, 64, kernel_size=3, padding=1)
+        self.pool = nn.AdaptiveAvgPool2d((2, 2))  # Output: 64 * 2 * 2 = 256
+        self.fc1 = nn.Linear(512, 128)  # BUG: expects 512 but conv outputs 256
+        self.fc2 = nn.Linear(128, num_classes)
         self.num_classes = num_classes
 
     def forward(self, x):
         x = F.relu(self.conv1(x))
+        x = F.relu(self.conv2(x))
+        x = self.pool(x)
+        x = x.view(x.size(0), -1)  # Flatten: [batch, 256]
+        x = F.relu(self.fc1(x))    # Will crash: 256 != 512
+        x = self.fc2(x)
         return x
 
+    def predict_with_default(self, x):
+        """Run prediction, return zeros if batch is empty."""
+        if x.size(0) == 0:
+            return torch.zeros(0, self.num_classes)  # BUG: no device specified
+        logits = self.forward(x)
+        return F.softmax(logits, dim=1)
+
 
 class Trainer:
     def __init__(self, model, optimizer):
file_context:
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import torch.nn.functional as F


class ImageClassifier(nn.Module):
    """Simple CNN image classifier."""

    def __init__(self, num_classes=10):
        super().__init__()
        self.conv1 = nn.Conv2d(3, 32, kernel_size=3, padding=1)
        self.conv2 = nn.Conv2d(32, 64, kernel_size=3, padding=1)
        self.pool = nn.AdaptiveAvgPool2d((2, 2))  # Output: 64 * 2 * 2 = 256
        self.fc1 = nn.Linear(512, 128)  # BUG: expects 512 but conv outputs 256
        self.fc2 = nn.Linear(128, num_classes)
        self.num_classes = num_classes

    def forward(self, x):
        x = F.relu(self.conv1(x))
        x = F.relu(self.conv2(x))
        x = self.pool(x)
        x = x.view(x.size(0), -1)  # Flatten: [batch, 256]
        x = F.relu(self.fc1(x))    # Will crash: 256 != 512
        x = self.fc2(x)
        return x

    def predict_with_default(self, x):
        """Run prediction, return zeros if batch is empty."""
        if x.size(0) == 0:
            return torch.zeros(0, self.num_classes)  # BUG: no device specified
        logits = self.forward(x)
        return F.softmax(logits, dim=1)


class Trainer:
    def __init__(self, model, optimizer):
        self.model = model
        self.optimizer = optimizer

gold_issues.description: Tensor shape mismatch: nn.Linear(512, 128) expects 512 input features but the preceding AdaptiveAvgPool2d((2,2)) with 64 channels produces 64*2*2=256 features. Will crash with RuntimeError at forward pass.
Device placement bug: torch.zeros() defaults to CPU but model may be on GPU. Should use device=x.device to match the input tensor's device.
